title: Hall of Fame
subtitle: A placeholder for exceptionally good and noteworthy products, services, courses, and tools that demonstrate real craft.
body:
This page is reserved for highlighting work that deserves recognition: products, services, courses, and tools that show unusual quality, discipline, usefulness, or integrity.

It starts with a single seeded entry and is intentionally structured as a placeholder for future Guild commentary and curation.

Current Entry

The first placeholder entry in the Hall of Fame.

Developer Tool AI Coding Agent Repository Workflow

Claude Code

Included here as the first placeholder Hall of Fame entry for a tool that is widely recognized for being useful in real codebases rather than merely impressive in demo form.

Its strengths as a coding workflow tool include strong repository awareness, pragmatic editing behavior, and a bias toward getting concrete engineering work done instead of drifting into pure theatrics. That makes it a fitting initial example of the kind of tooling this page is meant to celebrate.

Future Entries

The product slot is now live. Additional service and course entries remain open for future Guild recognition.

HUSHBELL

The doorbell dogs can't hear: a humane product concept built around minimizing startle while still alerting the human.

Service Slot

Reserved for a service that consistently delivers substance over hype.

Course Slot

Reserved for educational work that teaches with rigor and practical value.

See the Counterpoint

The Guild can recognize excellence and also document examples of poor, annoying, or damaging execution.

View Hall of Shame Back to Home
